verdict_reasoning: B2M is a non-TM light-chain subunit of MHC class I molecules. Although it has no transmembrane domain itself, B2M is a constitutive, stable non-covalent component of the MHC class I heterotrimer (α1/α2/α3 + β2M), assembled in the ER and co-trafficked to the cell surface as an integral part of the multi-subunit complex. The protein body of MHC-I includes β2M; it is not merely presented *by* the complex but is a structural element of the surface receptor itself. B2M associates with multiple TM-anchored MHC and MHC-like molecules (classical MHC-I, CD1, MR1, FcRn, HLA-E/F/G), ensuring robust surface accessibility across most nucleated cell types.
